seoTitle: How to Deploy a GitHub Project on Vercel for Free Hosting
metaDescription: Follow this step-by-step guide to deploy a GitHub project on Vercel for free, from uploading files to GitHub through to a live, hosted site.
intro: Vercel makes it possible to take a project sitting in a GitHub repository and turn it into a live, hosted website in just a few clicks, with no backend required. This guide walks through uploading a project to GitHub, connecting it to Vercel, and configuring the deployment settings, using a simple to-do list app as the example. It applies just as well to React apps, Next.js projects, or any static site you want hosted for free.

steps.heading: Upload your project to GitHub
steps.body: Create a new GitHub repository, then upload your project files, such as an index.html file, and commit the changes.

steps.heading: Connect GitHub to Vercel
steps.body: Go to Vercel, start deploying, and continue with GitHub to authenticate and link your GitHub account.

steps.heading: Import your repository
steps.body: Choose to grant Vercel access to specific repositories, select the one you just created, and install the connection.

steps.heading: Configure your project settings
steps.body: Set the project name and choose the correct framework preset, such as Other for a plain HTML project, before clicking Deploy.

steps.heading: View and manage your deployed site
steps.body: Once deployment finishes, visit your live project URL, and use the Vercel dashboard to manage logs, domains, and future deployments.

faq.question: Is deploying a GitHub project on Vercel really free?
faq.answer: Yes, Vercel offers free hosting for personal and small projects deployed directly from GitHub.

faq.question: Does Vercel automatically update my site when I push new code?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, any push to your GitHub repository triggers an automatic redeployment on Vercel.

faq.question: Can I deploy a React or Next.js project the same way?
faq.answer: Yes, the same GitHub-to-Vercel connection process works for React, Next.js, and other front-end frameworks, just with a different framework preset selected.

faq.question: Can I add a custom domain to my Vercel deployment?
faq.answer: Yes, custom domain settings are available from the Vercel project dashboard after your site is deployed.
